title: Service entrypoint contract
implementation_summary: Added the control-plane Claude review Service entrypoint contract for RP00.P02.M04.S01.
The S01 policy, module descriptor, validator, synthetic fixture, contract definition, README, and RP00 validator bind a frozen declaration-only Claude review entrypoint after RP00.P02.M03.S20.
The contract depends on the RP00.P02.M03.S20 Hermes Validation denied-path closeout being production_ready and accepts only the canonical S20 closeout input shape.

The contract defines 10 review contexts covering architecture, security, missing tests, validation theater, AI write boundaries, go/no-go, P0/P1 disposition, P2 disposition, human approval boundary, and blocked-claim context.
The service module exposes CONTROL_PLANE_CLAUDE_REVIEW_SERVICE_MODULE and getControlPlaneClaudeReviewServiceEntrypointContract as assert-then-return frozen metadata surfaces.
The validator locks required evidence refs, schema expectation fields, acceptance gates, future subphase handoffs, model claude-opus-4-8, effort max, and read-only review semantics.

The dedicated service tests prove the contract binds RP00.P02.M04.S01, declares review contexts, rejects drift, keeps the M00 three canonical runtime entrypoints unchanged, and mirrors the synthetic fixture.

The boundary is declaration-only: it does not execute service logic, create runtime routes, invoke Claude at runtime, write review queues, write review assignments, send review notifications, append audit ledgers, write audit events, mutate registries, replace human approval, write storage, write databases, or persist product state.
RP00.P02.M04 remains open after S01 and hands off to RP00.P02.M04.S02 Request normalization; RP00.P02 and RP00 remain open.
